name: Sauteed Minted Lamb
recipeIngredient: 3/4 lb Lamb steak, cut from leg
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove excess fat from the lamb.
2. If the piece is thick, cut it in half lengthwise so that each piece is about 3/4 inch thick.
3. Mix together red wine, vinegar, rosemary, and garlic in a small bowl.
4. Poke several holes at varying intervals on the lamb with a fork.
5. Place the lamb and sauce mixture in a zipper-lock bag and marinate for 20 minutes, turning after 10 minutes.
6. Heat a medium-size non-stick pan over medium heat.
7. Remove the lamb from the marinade and pat dry with paper towels. Reserve the marinade.
8. Brown the meat in the hot pan, sauteing 3 minutes per side. Transfer to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm.
9. Add the reserved marinade to the pan, ensuring it comes to a boil. Scrape up any brown bits from the bottom of the pan.
10. Add the mint jelly and heat through. Serve with the sauce spooned over top.
